rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF PHILIP CACHO SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the order settling the 6th account was filed on 1/24/19, not 1/28/19, the original payee of the life supporting equipment should have been identified on the disbursements schedule, it appears that 200, not 2000, shares of Micron Technology were purchased on 3/20/20 as shown on the informational schedule, and the Court can only order what is disclosed in a verified pleading and requested in the prayer, so if the successor trustee seeks confirmation or approval of his actions, he should include that in the prayer. An order, until it is signed, is a proposed order; there is no need to call it a [proposed] order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN SASSO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/20. No response as of 17:03 on 11/18/20 to the preliminary tentative ruling posted on 11/16/20. The supplement filed on 11/6/20 simply named Barry Goldberg; his age (adult, presumably), relationship to the decedent, and his address were not set forth, as they should have been had he been listed in item 8 of the petition. Who were Decedent's parents, through whom the niece and nephew take or would have taken, and when did Decedent's parents die? The proof of service filed on 11/5/20 was verified on 11/4/20, BEFORE the notice was reportedly mailed on 11/5/20. Either the verification is untrue or the date of mailing is inaccurate. The Notice also showed the wrong case number.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DARLENE FULLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/20. (No earlier availability.) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.26 D.3. A verified second supplement to the petition must be filed to provide the information regarding the heirship of the niece and nephews who are reportedly the only heirs. (Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Who were her siblings and when did they die and whose children are these niblings?) No courtesy copy of the proof of publication was provided. It was reviewed online as a one-time courtesy. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.93 G.] Courtesy copies of all papers filed going forward must be provided at least ONE WEEK BEFORE the hearing to avoid an automatic continuance for lack of courtesy copies.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FUMIKO TANAKA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Hearing issues have been e-mailed to counsel. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.
